prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `a644e277`.
codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `a644e277` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a644e277/__init__.py#L1), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a644e277/verifier.py#L4), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a644e277/generator.py#L4), and [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a644e277/notes.md#L1). The rule is: find the separator rows and columns, keep the ones that contain background-colored gaps at separator intersections, and crop the original input from the first marked row/column to the last marked row/column, inclusive.

I discarded the `sonnet` hint because it described compression rather than a literal crop, and I corrected the `opus` hint in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_a644e277/notes.md#L1). The generator matches the task family by explicitly building the separator lattice, placing the marked gaps on two horizontal and two vertical frontiers, scattering sparse dot noise in the tile interiors, and deriving the output as the exact crop. No new primitive was needed.

Validation passed end to end: official verifier `3/3`, generated verifier `1000/1000`, build artifacts created, and a preview sheet was checked visually against the originals. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a644e277/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a644e277/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a644e277/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a644e277/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/a644e277/previews/preview_03.png).
